speaker: DOCTOR
text: Good morning, Mr. Johnson. What brings you in today?

speaker: PATIENT
text: I've been having this pressure in my chest. It started about two hours ago.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Can you describe what it feels like?

speaker: PATIENT
text: Like something heavy sitting on my chest. Dull, not sharp.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Does it radiate anywhere — your arm, jaw, back?

speaker: PATIENT
text: A little into my left arm, and up to my jaw.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: On a scale of one to ten, how bad is it right now?

speaker: PATIENT
text: Around a six. It was worse when I was walking to the car.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Anything make it better or worse?

speaker: PATIENT
text: It got better when I sat down. Worse with walking.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Any shortness of breath, sweating, or nausea?

speaker: PATIENT
text: I felt sweaty at first. A little short of breath too.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: You said it started two hours ago — was this the first episode, or have you felt it before?

speaker: PATIENT
text: Actually, now that I think about it, I had something similar yesterday evening after dinner. It went away in a few minutes so I didn't mention it.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Got it. Any history of heart disease, high blood pressure, or diabetes?

speaker: PATIENT
text: High blood pressure for about ten years. My father had a heart attack at fifty-eight.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: What medications are you on?

speaker: PATIENT
text: Amlodipine for my blood pressure, atorvastatin for cholesterol, and a daily aspirin.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Any smoking or alcohol?

speaker: PATIENT
text: I smoked a pack a day for thirty years, quit five years ago.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: I want to get an ECG and a troponin, and apply the HEART score to assess your risk.
